questions: A car accelerates from 10 m/s to 30 m/s in 5 seconds. What is its acceleration?
A force of 50 Newtons is applied to a 10 kg object. What is the acceleration?
An object changes velocity from 5 m/s to 15 m/s while traveling 40 meters. What is its acceleration?
A wheel accelerates from 2 rad/s to 8 rad/s in 3 seconds. What is its angular acceleration?
A torque of 20 N·m is applied to an object with moment of inertia 5 kg·m². What is the angular acceleration?
An object's angular velocity changes by 12 rad/s in 4 seconds. What is its angular acceleration?
A system oscillates at frequency 2 Hz. What is its angular frequency?
An object rotates with angular velocity 5 rad/s. What is its angular frequency?
A spring-mass system has spring constant 100 N/m and mass 4 kg. What is its angular frequency?
An LC circuit has inductance 0.01 H and capacitance 0.0001 F. What is its angular frequency?
An object with moment of inertia 6 kg·m² rotates at 4 rad/s. What is its angular momentum?
A 3 kg object moves at 8 m/s in a circle of radius 1.5 m. What is its angular momentum?
An object has linear momentum of 20 kg·m/s and moves in a circle of radius 2 m. What is its angular momentum?
A wheel rotates through 2π radians in 4 seconds. What is its angular velocity?
An object rotates at 3 Hz. What is its angular velocity?
A point on a wheel with radius 0.5 m has a linear velocity of 10 m/s. What is the angular velocity?
A rectangle has length 5 m and width 3 m. What is its area?
A circle has radius 2 m. What is its area?
A triangle has base 6 m and height 4 m. What is its area?
A force of 1000 N creates pressure 50000 Pa. What is the area?
An object displaces 0.002 m³ of water (density 1000 kg/m³). What is the buoyant force? (Use g = 9.8 m/s²)
An object displaces fluid with weight 19.6 N. What is the buoyant force?
An object with volume 0.001 m³ is submerged in water (density 1000 kg/m³). What is the buoyant force? (Use g = 9.8 m/s²)
A capacitor stores 0.001 C of charge at 10 V. What is its capacitance?
A parallel plate capacitor has permittivity 8.85e-12 F/m, area 0.01 m², and plate separation 0.001 m. What is its capacitance?
A capacitor stores 0.05 J of energy at 20 V. What is its capacitance?
An object moves at 10 m/s in a circle of radius 5 m. What is its centripetal acceleration?
An object rotates with angular velocity 3 rad/s at radius 2 m. What is its centripetal acceleration?
A centripetal force of 50 N acts on a 5 kg object. What is the centripetal acceleration?
A 2 kg object experiences centripetal acceleration of 5 m/s². What is the centripetal force?
A 4 kg object moves at 6 m/s in a circle of radius 3 m. What is the centripetal force?
A 3 kg object rotates with angular velocity 2 rad/s at radius 2 m. What is the centripetal force?
A circuit has voltage 24 V and resistance 8 Ω. What is the current?
10 Coulombs of charge flows in 2 seconds. What is the current?
A device uses 120 W with voltage 12 V. What is the current?
A resistor dissipates 50 W with resistance 2 Ω. What is the current?
A substance has mass 5 kg and volume 0.002 m³. What is its density?
A gas has pressure 101325 Pa at temperature 300 K. What is its density? (Use R = 287 J/(kg·K))
An object moves from position 3 m to position 12 m. What is its displacement?
An object moves at 8 m/s for 5 seconds with an acceleration of 2 m/s². What is its displacement?
An object starts at 5 m/s and accelerates at 3 m/s² for 4 seconds. What is its displacement?
An object travels at 15 m/s for 4 seconds. What distance does it cover?
What is the distance between points (0, 0) and (3, 4)?
An object starts at 2 m/s and accelerates at 3 m/s² for 5 seconds. What distance does it travel?

A device has output power 50 W and input power 100 W. What is its efficiency?
A machine does 60 J of useful work with 80 J of input work. What is its efficiency?

A voltage of 100 V exists across a distance of 0.05 m. What is the electric field?

A charge of 0.002 C is at voltage 100 V. What is its electric potential energy?

A capacitor of 0.001 F is charged to 50 V. What is its stored energy?
A 5 kg object accelerates at 4 m/s². What is the force acting on it?
What is the weight of a 10 kg object on Earth where gravity is 9.8 m/s²?

A wave has a period of 0.5 seconds. What is its frequency?
An object rotates with angular velocity 4π rad/s. What is its frequency?
A wave travels at 340 m/s with wavelength 2 m. What is its frequency?
A system completes 60 cycles in 12 seconds. What is its frequency?

A surface has static coefficient 0.6 and normal force 50 N. What is the static friction force?
A surface has kinetic coefficient 0.4 and normal force 80 N. What is the kinetic friction force?

A voltage of 10 V is induced when current changes by 5 A in 0.1 seconds. What is the inductance?
A coil has self-inductance 0.05 H. What is its inductance?

If 100 Joules of work is done on an object, what is its kinetic energy?
A system has total energy of 150 J and potential energy of 50 J. What is the kinetic energy?
A charge of 0.001 C moving at 100 m/s experiences a force of 0.05 N. What is the magnetic field?

A magnetic flux of 0.02 Wb passes through an area of 0.01 m². What is the magnetic field?

A spring-mass system has mass 4 kg and spring constant 100 N/m. What is its period?
An object rotates with angular velocity π rad/s. What is its period?
A system completes 20 cycles in 10 seconds. What is its period?

A spring with spring constant 100 N/m is compressed 0.2 meters. What is its potential energy?
A system has total energy of 200 J and kinetic energy of 80 J. What is the potential energy?
1000 Joules of work is done in 5 seconds. What is the power?

An object has a velocity of -15 m/s. What is its speed?
